vocabulary.translation: I was just chatting with the neighbour.

vocabulary.example: 我们是邻居，应该互相帮助。
vocabulary.translation: We are neighbours; we should help each other.
She borrowed things from her neighbour.

vocabulary.example: 你能把音乐的声音调小一点吗？
vocabulary.translation: Can you turn the music volume down a little?

vocabulary.example: 是楼上邻居的音乐，声音太大了。
vocabulary.translation: It's the upstairs neighbour's music; it's way too loud.

vocabulary.english: To manage / To interfere / To mind
vocabulary.example: 每个人都应该管好自己的事。
vocabulary.translation: Everyone should take care of their own business.

vocabulary.example: 她总是爱管闲事，真让人烦。
vocabulary.translation: She always likes to meddle in other people's business; it's really annoying.
We are neighbours and have a good relationship.

vocabulary.example: 每个人都应该管好自己的事。
vocabulary.translation: Everyone should take care of their own business.

vocabulary.example: 我们是邻居，应该互相帮助。
vocabulary.translation: We are neighbours; we should help each other.

vocabulary.example: 她总是这样，而且每次都到很晚。
vocabulary.translation: She's always like this, and moreover it goes until very late every time.

vocabulary.english: Like to meddle / Always interfering in others' business

vocabulary.pinyin: yīn yuè shēng yīn tài dà

key_sentences.chinese: 她总是爱管闲事儿，真让人烦。
key_sentences.pinyin: Tā zǒng shì ài guǎn xián shì er, zhēn ràng rén fán.
key_sentences.english: She always likes to meddle in other people's business; it's really annoying.

key_sentences.chinese: 我刚才和邻居在聊天，没什么大事。
key_sentences.pinyin: Wǒ gāng cái hé lín jū zài liáo tiān, méi shén me dà shì.
key_sentences.english: I was just chatting with the neighbour; nothing important.

key_sentences.chinese: 你能把音乐的声音调小一点吗？
key_sentences.pinyin: Nǐ néng bǎ yīn yuè de shēng yīn tiáo xiǎo yì diǎn ma?
key_sentences.english: Can you turn the music volume down a little?

key_sentences.chinese: 我们是邻居，应该互相帮助。
key_sentences.pinyin: Wǒ men shì lín jū, yīng gāi hù xiāng bāng zhù.
key_sentences.english: We are neighbours; we should help each other.

key_sentences.chinese: 他不但聪明，而且非常努力。
key_sentences.pinyin: Tā bú dàn cōng míng, ér qiě fēi cháng nǔ lì.
key_sentences.english: He is not only smart, but also extremely hardworking.

key_sentences.chinese: 每个人都应该管好自己的事。
key_sentences.pinyin: Měi gè rén dōu yīng gāi guǎn hǎo zì jǐ de shì.
key_sentences.english: Everyone should take care of their own business.

grammar_points.title: 伴随结构 — Accompanying Structure with 跟/和
grammar_points.explanation: Subject + 跟/和 + Person + Verb = do something with someone. 我跟他聊天 = I chat with him. 她和邻居借东西 = She borrowed things from/with her neighbour. Note: 跟/和 can also link nouns meaning 'and': 他和她 = him and her. Context determines whether 跟/和 means 'with' or 'and'. When followed by a verb phrase it typically means 'with'.

examples.pinyin: Wǒ gēn lín jū zài liáo tiān.
Tā hé péng yǒu hù xiāng bāng zhù.
examples.english: She and her friend help each other.

exercises.question: What does 我跟他聊天 mean?
options: I and he are the same

exercises.question: How do you say 'She chats with her neighbour'?
option_pinyin: Tā gēn lín jū liáo tiān.
Tā lín jū gēn liáo tiān.
Gēn tā lín jū liáo tiān.
Tā liáo tiān lín jū gēn.
exercises.correct_pinyin: Tā gēn lín jū liáo tiān.

exercises.question: When 跟/和 is followed by a verb, it usually means:
Fill in: 我___邻居互相帮助。(My neighbour and I help each other.)
What does 她和邻居借东西 mean?
exercises.correct: She borrowed things from/with her neighbour
options: She borrowed things from/with her neighbour

grammar_points.title: 自己 — Reflexive Pronoun
grammar_points.explanation: 自己 means 'oneself / yourself / himself / herself' depending on context. It always refers back to the subject. 他自己做 = He does it himself. 照顾自己 = Take care of yourself. 自己的事 = One's own business. 自己 can stand alone or follow the subject pronoun for emphasis: 我自己来 = I'll do it myself.

examples.chinese: 每个人都要管好自己的事。
examples.pinyin: Měi gè rén dōu yào guǎn hǎo zì jǐ de shì.
examples.english: Everyone should manage their own affairs well.

examples.chinese: 他自己解决了这个问题。
examples.pinyin: Tā zì jǐ jiě jué le zhè gè wèn tí.
examples.english: He solved this problem by himself.

exercises.question: What does 他自己做 mean?
options: He wants others to do it

exercises.question: How do you say 'Take care of yourself'?
Fill in: 你要管好___的事，别管别人的。(Take care of your own business; don't mind others'.)
What does 我自己来 mean?

grammar_points.title: 而且 — Moreover / Furthermore
grammar_points.explanation: 而且 is used to add a further point, stronger than 也. Formula: Sentence 1, 而且 + Sentence 2. Often paired with 不但...而且 = not only...but also. 他聪明，而且努力 = He's smart, and moreover hardworking. 不但 goes before the first adjective/verb; 而且 introduces the second stronger point.

examples.chinese: 她不但爱管闲事，而且声音很大。
examples.pinyin: Tā bú dàn ài guǎn xián shì, ér qiě shēng yīn hěn dà.
examples.english: She not only likes to meddle, but moreover speaks very loudly.

examples.chinese: 这首音乐好听，而且让人放松。
examples.pinyin: Zhè shǒu yīn yuè hǎo tīng, ér qiě ràng rén fàng sōng.
examples.english: This piece of music sounds good, and moreover it helps people relax.

exercises.question: Which pattern uses 而且 correctly?
What does 不但...而且 mean?
Fill in: 她不但爱聊天，___很爱管闲事。(She not only loves chatting, but also loves meddling.)
How is 而且 stronger than 也?
exercises.correct: 而且 adds a further or more emphatic point; 也 simply adds an additional equal point
options: 而且 adds a further or more emphatic point; 也 simply adds an additional equal point
也 is stronger than 而且

quiz.question: What does 管 mean in this context?
quiz.correct: To manage / To interfere / To mind
options: To manage / To interfere / To mind

quiz.question: How do you say 'I'm chatting with my neighbour'?
What does 不但...而且 mean?
Which sentence uses 自己 correctly?
What does 爱管闲事 mean?
quiz.correct: Like to meddle / Always interfering in others' business
options: Like to meddle / Always interfering in others' business

quiz.question: What does 互相帮助 mean?
What does 音乐声音太大 mean?
What does 自己的事 mean?
What does 借给邻居 mean?

lines.speaker: A
lines.chinese: 加缪，你听到刚才那个声音了吗？
lines.pinyin: Jiā Miù, nǐ tīng dào gāng cái nà gè shēng yīn le ma?
lines.english: Camus, did you hear that sound just now?

lines.speaker: B
lines.chinese: 听到了，是楼上邻居的音乐，声音太大了。
lines.pinyin: Tīng dào le, shì lóu shàng lín jū de yīn yuè, shēng yīn tài dà le.
lines.english: Yes, it's the upstairs neighbour's music; it's way too loud.

lines.speaker: A
lines.chinese: 她总是这样，而且每次都到很晚。
lines.pinyin: Tā zǒng shì zhè yàng, ér qiě měi cì dōu dào hěn wǎn.
lines.english: She's always like this, and moreover it goes until very late every time.

lines.speaker: B
lines.chinese: 我去跟她说，叫她把声音调小一点。
lines.pinyin: Wǒ qù gēn tā shuō, jiào tā bǎ shēng yīn tiáo xiǎo yì diǎn.
lines.english: I'll go talk to her and ask her to turn the volume down a bit.

lines.speaker: A
lines.chinese: 算了，这是她自己的事，你别管闲事了。
lines.pinyin: Suàn le, zhè shì tā zì jǐ de shì, nǐ bié guǎn xián shì le.
lines.english: Forget it; it's her own business — don't meddle.

lines.speaker: B
lines.chinese: 但是我们是邻居，应该互相帮助，互相尊重嘛。
lines.pinyin: Dàn shì wǒ men shì lín jū, yīng gāi hù xiāng bāng zhù, hù xiāng zūn zhòng ma.
lines.english: But we're neighbours; we should help each other and respect each other.

lines.speaker: A
lines.chinese: 你知道吗，刚才王阿姨又来问我的事了。
lines.pinyin: Nǐ zhī dào ma, gāng cái Wáng ā yí yòu lái wèn wǒ de shì le.
lines.english: You know what, Auntie Wang just came to ask about my business again.

lines.speaker: B
lines.chinese: 她总是爱管闲事，你别在意。
lines.pinyin: Tā zǒng shì ài guǎn xián shì, nǐ bié zài yì.
lines.english: She always loves to meddle; don't take it to heart.

lines.speaker: A
lines.chinese: 而且她还跟其他邻居说我的事，真烦人。
lines.pinyin: Ér qiě tā hái gēn qí tā lín jū shuō wǒ de shì, zhēn fán rén.
lines.english: Moreover she's been telling other neighbours about my affairs — really annoying.

lines.speaker: B
lines.chinese: 没关系，我们只要管好自己的事就行了。
lines.pinyin: Méi guān xi, wǒ men zhǐ yào guǎn hǎo zì jǐ de shì jiù xíng le.
lines.english: Never mind — as long as we take care of our own business, it's fine.

lines.speaker: A
lines.chinese: 说得对。我们互相帮助，不管别人怎么说。
lines.pinyin: Shuō de duì. Wǒ men hù xiāng bāng zhù, bù guǎn bié rén zěn me shuō.
lines.english: You're right. We help each other and don't worry about what others say.
